title: torilis helvetica subsp. purpurea
intro: Torilis helvetica subsp. purpurea, also known as Purple Hemlock, is a subspecies of plant in the carrot family that is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant effects. The plant's leaves, flowers, and stems have been used to treat various health issues, such as respiratory problems, digestive issues, and skin conditions, due to its high content of saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. Herbal preparations of Torilis helvetica subsp. purpurea include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to promote relaxation, reduce inflammation, and support overall health.
